_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse how literary devices, language features and visual elements shape an audience's preferences about the representation of characters, events, settings and themes.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse how literary devices, language features and visual elements shape an audience's preferences about the representation of characters, events, settings and themes.
y: Understanding how audience preferences are shaped by textual techniques reveals the persuasive power of literature.
This analytical skill helps us recognise how we are being positioned as readers.
Applying this understanding makes us more intentional in our own creative and analytical writing.
eg: We analyse how an author uses literary devices to make a morally ambiguous character sympathetic.
We examine how language features position readers to prefer one interpretation of events over another.
We discuss how visual elements in graphic novels or films guide audience responses to settings and themes.
We compare how different literary techniques create different audience preferences for the same type of character.
We evaluate how effectively a text shapes audience preferences and whether this manipulation is ethically justified.
We create a text that deliberately uses literary techniques to shape the reader's preferences.
